title: AsyncRW blanket trait and boxed WsSink/WsStream lack clarity on necessity
description:
Lines 23-27 define AsyncRW (private blanket trait) and box WsSink/WsStream trait objects without explaining why. A future contributor reading ws_upgrade_and_auth<S: AsyncRW> must scroll to find the trait definition. The boxed sink/stream types incur vtable dispatch cost per message, but there's no comment indicating whether this is intentional (for future refactoring flexibility) or necessary (to handle heterogeneous stream types).

Clarify:
1. Why AsyncRW exists (is it necessary to unify TcpStream and Socks5Stream<TcpStream>? Could we use a single-monomorphized path instead?)
2. Whether the dynamic dispatch cost is acceptable or a performance footprint worth revisiting
3. Rename AsyncRW to AsyncReadWrite (more idiomatic) or inline its bounds if not widely used
